metadata.title:
A BANQUET Will Bf


GIVEN DALEY

pages.raw_text:
James ]. Daley, assistant city
comptroller, who is attending the
twenty-fourth annual meeting of the
national convention of the American
Federation of Labor in San Francisco,
writes to n lodge member that the
meeting is one of the most success
ful In the history of the order.


Mr.. Daley is a delegate from the
local federation. No. 11,62i, and is
one of the six to be elected from
this state. There are 500 delegates
present from every part of the
world. The speech delivered by
Michael McDavitt, member of the
house of parliament from Ireland,
was one of the most brilliant ever
given before labor organization in
America.


Hon. Sir Wignall of London moved
his audience to tears in defense of
woman's suffrage at meeting of the
union Saturday evening.


Upon Mr. Daley's return to Spo-
kane Federal union, No. 11,624, will
tender him banquet, at which he
will be expected to relate the pro-
ceedings of the convention.
